Search results: 



--- SOURCE 1: International migration flows data ---
URL: https://www.migrationdataportal.org/themes/international-migration-flows

SUMMARY:
Recent trends  The countries receiving the most new, permanent-type migrants in 2023 were the United States (1.2 million), the United Kingdom (a record 750,000, +52% from 2022), Germany (700,000, +4% from 2022), Canada (a record 470,000, +7.8%) and Spain (360,000, +12%) (ibid.).



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 2: Global Trends report 2023 | UNHCR US ---
URL: https://www.unhcr.org/us/global-trends-report-2023

SUMMARY:
At the end of 2023, 117.3 million people worldwide were forcibly displaced as a result of persecution, conflict, violence, human rights violations and events

--- SOURCE 3: Top 10 Migration Issues of 2023 | migrationpolicy.org ---
URL: https://www.migrationpolicy.org/programs/migration-information-source/top-10-migration-issues-2023

SUMMARY:
International migration in 2023 was shaped by a broad array of conflicts, labor and protection needs, and political dynamics, among other factors.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

--- SOURCE 5: International Migration Outlook 2023 - OECD ---
URL: https://www.oecd.org/en/publications/2023/10/international-migration-outlook-2023_0faed233.html

SUMMARY:
Permanent-type migration to OECD countries increased by 26% in 2022 compared with 2021. Preliminary figures for 2023 suggest a further increase. Family

--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://geopoliticalfutures.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/WG_World-Migration.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The illustration presents key data on global migration, highlighting the top 20 international migration corridors, origins, and destinations for 2020, alongside growth trends in international migration from 1990 to 2020, emphasizing significant patterns involving countries like India, the U.S., and various European nations.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://www.pewresearch.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/ft_22.12.16_global-migration_01.png
DESCRIPTION: The data presents the international migrant population in millions across various regions for 2020, highlighting Europe and Asia as the two largest hosts with 86.7 million and 85.6 million migrants respectively, followed by Northern America, Africa, Latin America and the Caribbean, and Oceania.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://joint-research-centre.ec.europa.eu/sites/default/files/styles/ewcms_metatag_image/public/2023-12/Atlas_of_Migration2023_K4P_1422x950.png?itok=Lso8qalN
DESCRIPTION: A cracked, dark gray surface features a colorful, abstract world map with the title "Atlas of Migration 2023" above it.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://menafn.com/updates/pr/2023-12/27/pr_14367_64ca1d4a_image_story.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A world map illustrates global human migration trends in 2023, showing countries with positive net migration in blue and negative net migration in red, based on recent data.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://pwonlyias.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/international-653a63715ada5-1568x882.webp
DESCRIPTION: A crowded airport check-in area is depicted, featuring travelers waiting in line with luggage, against a backdrop of displays and signage related to international air travel.

enriched_points.expanded_content: In 2023, over 117.3 million individuals were forcibly displaced due to conflicts and human rights violations, prompting significant migration patterns globally.
Rising migration to urban areas is driven by increased job opportunities and economic growth, with permanent migration to OECD countries up by 26% in 2022.

enriched_points.point_title: Climate change and its impact on migration
enriched_points.expanded_content: Climate change exacerbates displacement, as environmental factors lead to increased migration, adding pressure on urban infrastructures and communities worldwide.
